trips.name: Bernese Oberland Classic
trips.description: The Bernese Oberland is Switzerland's alpine heartland — a valley of thundering waterfalls, cable cars to 4,000m peaks, and the iconic trio of Eiger, Mönch and Jungfrau watching over every turn. The drive from Bern to Lauterbrunnen passes mirror-flat lakes before the valley walls close in dramatically.
trips.cover_search_query: Grindelwald Eiger mountain village Switzerland

trips.name: Lake Geneva & the Swiss Riviera
trips.description: The northern shore of Lake Geneva runs through some of the most manicured scenery in Europe — Belle Époque promenades, UNESCO vineyard terraces, and Chillon Castle rising from the water. Montreux's jazz heritage and Vevey's Charlie Chaplin connection make this as culturally rich as it is beautiful.
trips.cover_search_query: Château de Chillon Montreux Lake Geneva Switzerland

trips.name: Matterhorn & Zermatt Valley
trips.description: The drive up the Mattervispa valley to car-free Zermatt offers the most dramatic mountain reveal in the Alps — the Matterhorn first appearing as a dark pyramid above the valley walls. Saas-Fee's 360° glacier arena and Sion's twin hilltop fortresses provide the perfect bookends.
trips.cover_search_query: Matterhorn Zermatt reflection Switzerland

trips.name: Lucerne & Central Switzerland
trips.description: Lucerne's Chapel Bridge and the Lion Monument bookend one of Switzerland's most accessible alpine circuits. The Reuss valley road to Andermatt climbs through the Schöllenen Gorge — a dramatic slot canyon where the Devil's Bridge spans churning white water.
trips.cover_search_query: Chapel Bridge Lucerne Switzerland lake

trips.name: Engadin Valley & St. Moritz
trips.description: The Inn valley between Maloja and Zernez is Europe's sunniest alpine valley — 320 days of sunshine, frozen lakes in winter and wildflower meadows in summer. St. Moritz's gilded resort culture gives way rapidly to the windswept Silsersee, where Nietzsche found his philosophy of eternal recurrence.
trips.cover_search_query: St. Moritz lake Engadin valley Switzerland

trips.name: Ticino Lakeside
trips.description: South of the Alps, Switzerland turns Italian — terracotta rooftops, palm trees, and lake towns that feel more Lombardy than Lucerne. Bellinzona's three UNESCO castles give way to the magnolia-lined Locarno waterfront and Lugano's cosmopolitan energy.
trips.cover_search_query: Lugano lake Ticino Switzerland palm trees

trips.name: Furka & Grimsel Passes
trips.description: The Furka and Grimsel are twin passes that inspired Shelley's Frankenstein and appeared in a James Bond car chase — vertiginous hairpins above turquoise glacial lakes on both sides. Meiringen's Reichenbach Falls, where Sherlock Holmes met his fate, sets the dramatic tone.
trips.cover_search_query: Furka Pass mountain road hairpins Switzerland

trips.name: Rhine Falls & Northern Switzerland
trips.description: The Rhine Falls at Schaffhausen are Europe's largest waterfall by volume — a curtain of white water 150m wide that shakes the viewing platforms. The medieval town of Stein am Rhein has frescoed house fronts painted to look like carved stone, virtually unchanged since the 15th century.
highlights: Stein am Rhein painted medieval facades
trips.cover_search_query: Rhine Falls Schaffhausen waterfall Switzerland

trips.name: Appenzell & the Alpstein
trips.description: Appenzell Innerrhoden is the most traditional canton in Switzerland — men still carry ceremonial swords to the open-air parliament, and the flower meadows above are worked by families who have owned the same strips since the Middle Ages. The Säntis massif's jagged limestone ridgeline lords over the entire region.
trips.cover_search_query: Appenzell village colourful houses Switzerland Alps

trips.name: Gotthard Pass & Val Tremola
trips.description: The Gotthard is Switzerland's most historic mountain crossing — a cobbled old road (Val Tremola) that winds through 24 hairpins unchanged since the 18th century. The pass summit museum chronicles three centuries of alpine travel from mule caravans to the world's longest rail tunnel beneath it.
trips.cover_search_query: Gotthard Pass old road cobblestone Switzerland

trips.name: Bernina Pass & Poschiavo
trips.description: The Bernina Pass road follows Europe's highest non-rack railway crossing the Alps — a UNESCO World Heritage route that passes the frozen Lago Bianco and spirals down through the Brusio viaduct. The contrast between the snow-white pass and the palm trees of Poschiavo 1,800m below is startling.
trips.cover_search_query: Bernina Pass lake white Switzerland Graubünden

trips.name: Swiss National Park & Lower Engadin
trips.description: Switzerland's only national park protects 170km² of recovering alpine wilderness with ibex, golden eagles and red deer roaming freely without a single tourist infrastructure point inside. The Romansh-speaking villages of Guarda and Sent are living museums of medieval Engadin architecture.
trips.cover_search_query: Swiss National Park Engadin valley Switzerland

trips.name: Aletsch Glacier & Riederalp
trips.description: The Aletsch is Europe's largest glacier — a 23km river of ice flowing from the Jungfrau massif into the Rhône valley, inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage site. The car-free villages of Riederalp and Bettmeralp sit above the tree line with view terraces looking directly onto the glacier's fractured surface.
trips.cover_search_query: Aletsch Glacier aerial view Switzerland UNESCO

trips.name: Lavaux Vineyard Terraces
trips.description: The Lavaux vineyard terraces above Lake Geneva are a UNESCO World Heritage landscape — 2,000 years of Roman, monastic and peasant viticulture carved into south-facing hillsides that plunge to the lakeshore. On hot summer afternoons the lake reflects the terraces in a mirror of gold and blue.
trips.cover_search_query: Lavaux vineyard terraces Lake Geneva Switzerland UNESCO

trips.name: Fribourg & Gruyères
trips.description: Gruyères castle lords over one of Switzerland's best-preserved medieval towns, and the cheese of the same name has been made in the valley below for nearly 900 years. Fribourg's old town bridges the linguistic divide between German and French Switzerland on a cliff peninsula above the Sarine river.
trips.cover_search_query: Gruyères medieval castle town Switzerland

trips.name: Saas Valley & Fee Glacier
trips.description: The Saas valley is Zermatt's quieter, equally spectacular neighbour — enclosed on three sides by 4,000m peaks and the Fee Glacier, the car-free village of Saas-Fee has been traffic-free since 1951. The world's highest outdoor revolving cable car deposits you at the edge of a glacier at 3,600m.
highlights: Fee Glacier world's highest ice rink
trips.cover_search_query: Saas-Fee glacier village Switzerland Alps

trips.name: Simmental & Gstaad
trips.description: The Simmental valley runs through the heart of the Bernese Prealps, connecting the lake shores to Gstaad's rarefied resort world of horse-drawn sleighs and private banks. Château-d'Oex hosts the world's most celebrated hot air balloon festival each January, filling the valley with colour.
highlights: Rossinière Grand Chalet largest wooden house
trips.cover_search_query: Gstaad alpine village Switzerland mountains

trips.name: Walensee & Heidiland
trips.description: The Walensee is one of the most dramatic drives in Switzerland — a narrow lake hemmed between vertical limestone walls, the road barely clinging to the cliff face above turquoise water. Maienfeld is the actual village that inspired Johanna Spyri's Heidi, with the original farmhouse still receiving pilgrims.
trips.cover_search_query: Walensee lake cliffs Switzerland dramatic

trips.name: Valais Wine Route
trips.description: The Valais is Switzerland's sunniest and most wine-productive canton — sheltered by the Alps, its dry continental climate produces some of Europe's most distinctive whites from ancient indigenous grape varieties like Petite Arvine and Amigne found nowhere else. The Salgesch UNESCO vineyards are the sunniest spot in Switzerland.
highlights: Sion twin fortresses Valère and Tourbillon
trips.cover_search_query: Valais vineyard terraces Switzerland Rhône valley

trips.name: Susten & Klausen Pass Circuit
trips.description: The Susten and Klausen passes form a back-to-back alpine circuit that most tourists miss entirely — high-altitude moorland, waterfall valleys and dramatic switchbacks above the treeline. Meiringen's Reichenbach Falls, where Sherlock Holmes met his fate, and Glarus's story bookend a forgotten Switzerland.
trips.cover_search_query: Susten Pass alpine road Switzerland

trips.name: Neuchâtel & the Swiss Jura
trips.description: The Swiss Jura is watchmaking country — La Chaux-de-Fonds and Le Locle were rebuilt on a grid plan after fire specifically to allow maximum northern light into watchmakers' workshops, and the entire twin city is UNESCO listed. The rolling limestone plateau above the lakes feels like a different Switzerland entirely.
trips.cover_search_query: Neuchâtel lake old town castle Switzerland

trips.name: Murten & the Three Lakes
trips.description: Switzerland's three western lakes — Biel, Murten and Neuchâtel — are linked by canals created in a 19th-century land reclamation project that drained vast marshes. Murten is the best-preserved medieval walled town in Switzerland, its towers and rampart walk unchanged since the Battle of Murten in 1476.
trips.cover_search_query: Murten medieval walled town Switzerland lake

trips.name: Prättigau, Davos & Arosa
trips.description: The Prättigau valley is the secret entry into Graubünden's alpine heartland — a narrowing road between hayfield farms and Walser villages that suddenly opens into Klosters's discreet luxury. Davos hosts the World Economic Forum each January, but in summer it reveals a long blue lake beneath glacier-streaked peaks.
trips.cover_search_query: Davos lake Switzerland mountains summer

trips.name: Obergoms & Binn Valley
trips.description: The upper Rhône valley above Brig is the Goms — a string of tiny Walser villages clinging to south-facing slopes above the growing glacier river. The Binn Valley is Switzerland's most mineral-rich protected landscape, where Victorian gem-hunters collected rare crystals and the tradition quietly continues today.
trips.cover_search_query: Goms valley Switzerland winter Walser villages

trips.name: Zurich, Lake Zurich & Einsiedeln
trips.description: Lake Zurich's eastern shore is the Gold Coast — affluent villages with manicured gardens above turquoise water, ending at Rapperswil's rose-covered castle. Einsiedeln's baroque monastery is the most visited pilgrimage site in Switzerland, its black Madonna drawing 150,000 pilgrims each year on a journey from medieval Europe.
trips.cover_search_query: Rapperswil castle Lake Zurich Switzerland
